title: verbena bracteata f. albiflora
intro: Verbena bracteata f. albiflora, also known as white verbena, is a perennial herb in the family Verbenaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin infections, and fever. The health benefits of white verbena include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve stress and anxiety, and promote a good night's sleep. White verbena can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe digestive issues and calm the mind.
